narrative:
Former President Bill Clinton testified under oath to the House Oversight Committee last week regarding his relationship with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, with video of the depositions released Monday [1]. Clinton's testimony spanned over two days and lasted four-and-a-half hours [2].

## Key Claims and Timeline

Clinton testified that he first met Epstein in 2002 when he boarded the financier's private jet for humanitarian work, but their relationship ended in 2003 [1]. Clinton acknowledged taking "at least 26 times" on Epstein's aircraft, many as part of his global AIDS initiative [2]. He stated he took his final flight on what's been dubbed the "Lolita Express" in 2003, five years before Epstein's 2008 guilty plea [2].

Regarding his knowledge of Epstein's crimes, Clinton testified: "There's nothing that I saw when I was around him that made me realize he was trafficking women" [1]. He said he first learned of Epstein's sex crimes in 2008 when Epstein pleaded guilty [2].

## Hot Tub Photo Questioning

A significant portion of Clinton's testimony focused on photographs from the Justice Department's Epstein files, including one showing Clinton in a hot tub with an unidentified woman whose face was redacted [2]. Clinton testified the photo was taken in Brunei and said "I had forgotten that there was anybody in the hot tub" and "I don't know who that is" [2]. When asked directly if he engaged in sexual activity with the woman, Clinton responded "No" [2].

Clinton described the setting as a hotel pool area next to suites where his team was staying "on invitation from the sultan of Brunei," with a Secret Service agent present "at the other end of the pool" [2]. He testified that no minors were present in the pool area [2].

## Trump Connection

Clinton also recounted a conversation with Donald Trump where Trump allegedly told him he had "some great times" with Epstein [1]. According to Clinton's testimony, Trump said "You know, we had some great times together over the years, but we fell out all because of a real estate deal" [1]. Clinton added that Trump "never said anything to me to make me think he was involved in anything improper with regard to Epstein" [1].

## Hillary Clinton's Testimony

Hillary Clinton provided her own deposition a day earlier, telling the committee she does not remember meeting Epstein [1]. The sources note that neither Clinton has been accused of wrongdoing [1].

citations.title: Bill Clinton denies any knowledge of Epstein's sex crimes

keyDevelopments: House Oversight Committee releases video of Bill Clinton's sworn testimony about Jeffrey Epstein relationship
Clinton denies knowledge of Epstein's sex trafficking activities, claims relationship ended in 2003
Clinton questioned about redacted hot tub photo, denies sexual activity with unidentified woman
Clinton reveals Trump told him about having 'great times' with Epstein
Hillary Clinton testified separately, claims no memory of meeting Epstein
